did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper is a narrative review summarizing studies that used machine learning algorithms (MLAs), deep learning (DL), and computer vision to analyze dermatoscopic images for predicting melanoma prognosis. Specific AI methods mentioned include pre-trained ResNet50, Foundation models (Vision Transformers/ViT), semi-supervised learning, transfer learning (VGG-16, DenseNet121, EfficientNet), and explainable AI (XAI). The dermatology tasks include predicting metastasis, Breslow thickness, and histopathological subtypes.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The title explicitly states 'A narrative review of the literature'. The abstract describes a 'comprehensive literature search' conducted in electronic databases to identify and summarize existing studies on machine learning algorithms for melanoma prognosis, rather than presenting new primary experimental data. The methods section details the search strategy and inclusion criteria for selecting prior studies.

non_compliance_reason: The paper is a review, survey, or meta-analysis rather than an original research paper.
